notes: Catchall count corrected to 70 per EDA (spec states 46, EDA counted 70). Employment change/pct sign consistency is intentionally NOT enforced (12 BLS rounding mismatches). Wage lower bound set to $25,000 (wider than EDA min $30,160 for robustness).

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bls_ooh GROUP BY soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per soc_code. Any duplicate SOC code indicates a transformer or promote failure. This is the structural foundation of the table.
rules.rationale: EDA: 832 distinct soc_code values across 832 rows (100% unique). Zero duplicates observed. Logical model declares soc_code as the natural key with zero-duplicate constraint.

rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Every soc_code must match the SOC standard format XX-XXXX (2-digit major group, hyphen, 4-digit detail). This is the primary join key for O*NET and the CIP-SOC crosswalk.
rules.rationale: EDA: All 832 rows match ^\d{2}-\d{4}$ with zero violations. Domain context confirms SOC 2018 XX-XXXX format as the authoritative standard.

rules.name: Row count exactly 832
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 832 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.bls_ooh
rules.description: Silver transformation is 1:1 with Bronze. No rows should be added or dropped. Exact count of 832 must be preserved.
rules.rationale: EDA: 832 rows in Bronze source. Spec confirms 1:1 row mapping with no filtering or aggregation. Any deviation indicates a transformer bug.

rules.name: SOC major group: valid 22 codes
rules.description: The soc_major_group field (first 2 characters of soc_code) must be one of the 22 valid SOC major group codes defined in the spec.
rules.rationale: EDA: All 22 major groups present in the data. Zero SOC codes outside these 22 groups. Derived deterministically from soc_code[:2].

rules.name: SOC major group name: referential integrity with soc_major_group
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE soc_major_group_name IS NULL OR TRIM(soc_major_group_name) = ''
rules.description: Every row must have a non-null, non-empty soc_major_group_name. This is derived via lookup from soc_major_group, so all 22 valid group codes must resolve to a name.
rules.rationale: EDA: All 22 SOC major groups present with names confirmed in the spec lookup table. Logical model marks soc_major_group_name as NOT NULL.

rules.name: SOC major group derived correctly from soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE soc_major_group != SUBSTRING(soc_code, 1, 2)
rules.description: The soc_major_group field must equal the first 2 characters of soc_code. Validates the derivation logic.
rules.rationale: Logical model derivation rule: soc_major_group = soc_code[:2]. Must hold for all 832 rows.

rules.name: record_id not null and unique
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bls_ooh GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic surrogate primary key computed via compute_grain_id(). It must be non-null and unique across all rows.
rules.rationale: Logical model: record_id is PK, NOT NULL. Derived via compute_grain_id(row, ['soc_code'], prefix='ooh'). SHA-256 hash should produce 0 collisions for 832 rows.

rules.name: soc_code not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: soc_code is the natural key and must be present in every row.
rules.rationale: EDA: 0 nulls in soc_code across 832 rows (0.0% null rate). Logical model marks as NOT NULL.

rules.name: occupation_title not null or empty
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE occupation_title IS NULL OR TRIM(occupation_title) = ''
rules.description: occupation_title is a required field used for catchall_flag derivation and human display.
rules.rationale: EDA: 0 nulls in occupation_title across 832 rows. 832 distinct values (1:1 with soc_code). Logical model marks as NOT NULL.

rules.name: broad_occupation_flag: exactly 7 True
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN broad_occupation_flag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 7 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.bls_ooh
rules.description: Exactly 7 SOC codes should be flagged as broad/rolled-up occupations. These are hardcoded from the Bronze SOC audit: 13-1020, 13-2020, 29-2010, 31-1120, 39-7010, 47-4090, 51-2090.
rules.rationale: EDA: All 7 known broad codes confirmed present. Spec hardcodes the list. Any deviation means the hardcoded list or source data changed.

rules.name: catchall_flag: exactly 70 True
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN catchall_flag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 70 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.bls_ooh
rules.description: Exactly 70 occupations should have catchall_flag=True. These are occupations with 'all other' in the title (case-insensitive substring match).
rules.rationale: EDA CORRECTION: Spec states 46 but EDA counted 70 titles containing 'all other' (case-insensitive). All 70 confirmed. One edge case: 29-1029 'Dentists, all other specialists' has 'all other' mid-title. Threshold set to 70 per EDA evidence.

rules.name: No broad+catchall overlap
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE broad_occupation_flag = true AND catchall_flag = true
rules.description: None of the 7 broad occupation codes should also be catchall categories. The flags are independent classifications with no expected overlap.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'No overlap between broad occupation codes and catchall categories. None of the 7 known broad codes contain all other in their titles.' 0 overlap confirmed.

rules.name: broad_occupation_flag not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE broad_occupation_flag IS NULL
rules.description: broad_occupation_flag is a derived NOT NULL boolean. Must be True or False for every row.
rules.rationale: Logical model: broad_occupation_flag is NOT NULL boolean. Derived from hardcoded SOC list, so always deterministic.

rules.name: catchall_flag not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE catchall_flag IS NULL
rules.description: catchall_flag is a derived NOT NULL boolean. Must be True or False for every row.
rules.rationale: Logical model: catchall_flag is NOT NULL boolean. Derived from occupation_title substring match, always deterministic.

rules.name: employment_current positive when not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE employment_current IS NOT NULL AND employment_current <= 0
rules.description: Employment current must be a positive count when present. Zero or negative employment is impossible for an active BLS occupation.
rules.rationale: EDA: Min 200, all values positive. 0 nulls across 832 rows. Logical model: 'Positive when present.'

rules.name: employment_projected positive when not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE employment_projected IS NOT NULL AND employment_projected <= 0
rules.description: Employment projected must be a positive count when present. Zero or negative projected employment is impossible.
rules.rationale: EDA: Min 200, all values positive. 0 nulls across 832 rows.

rules.name: employment_change_pct range: -50.0 to +60.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE employment_change_pct IS NOT NULL AND (employment_change_pct < -50.0 OR employment_change_pct > 60.0)
rules.description: Employment change percentage must fall within -50.0 to +60.0. This is a tight sanity bound based on actual data range, with headroom for future variation.
rules.rationale: EDA: Actual range -36.1 to 49.9. Spec suggested -100 to +200 but that is too loose. Bounds set at -50 to +60 to catch real anomalies while allowing ~40% headroom beyond observed extremes.

rules.name: openings_annual_avg non-negative
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE openings_annual_avg IS NOT NULL AND openings_annual_avg < 0
rules.description: Annual average openings must be >= 0. Four occupations have zero due to BLS rounding, which is valid.
rules.rationale: EDA: Min 0, 4 rows with openings_annual_avg=0 (BLS rounding artifact). Negative values are impossible for job openings.

rules.name: growth_category valid enum values
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE growth_category NOT IN ('declining_fast', 'declining', 'stable', 'growing', 'growing_fast', 'booming') AND growth_category IS NOT NULL
rules.description: growth_category must be one of the 6 valid enum values when not null. Null is only valid when employment_change_pct is null.
rules.rationale: EDA: All 832 rows assigned to one of 6 categories. Distribution: declining_fast=54, declining=155, stable=80, growing=481, growing_fast=51, booming=11. Sum=832, no nulls.

rules.name: growth_category null iff employment_change_pct is null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE (growth_category IS NULL AND employment_change_pct IS NOT NULL) OR (growth_category IS NOT NULL AND employment_change_pct IS NULL)
rules.description: growth_category must be null exactly when employment_change_pct is null, and non-null exactly when employment_change_pct is non-null. They are logically coupled.
rules.rationale: EDA: 0 nulls for both fields in current data. Spec derivation rule: growth_category is null only when employment_change_pct is null. Rule validates the bidirectional null relationship.

rules.name: growth_category matches employment_change_pct bucket
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE employment_change_pct IS NOT NULL AND growth_category != CASE WHEN employment_change_pct < -10.0 THEN 'declining_fast' WHEN employment_change_pct >= -10.0 AND employment_change_pct < -1.0 THEN 'declining' WHEN employment_change_pct >= -1.0 AND employment_change_pct < 1.0 THEN 'stable' WHEN employment_change_pct >= 1.0 AND employment_change_pct < 10.0 THEN 'growing' WHEN employment_change_pct >= 10.0 AND employment_change_pct < 20.0 THEN 'growing_fast' WHEN employment_change_pct >= 20.0 THEN 'booming' END
rules.description: Validates that growth_category is derived correctly from employment_change_pct using the spec-defined half-open interval thresholds. Critical because boundary rows exist at every threshold.
rules.rationale: EDA: 19 boundary rows (2.3%) sit exactly on threshold values. Distribution verified: declining_fast=54, declining=155, stable=80, growing=481, growing_fast=51, booming=11. Half-open intervals must be precise.

rules.name: median_annual_wage range: $25,000-$250,000 when not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL AND (median_annual_wage < 25000 OR median_annual_wage > 250000)
rules.description: When present, median_annual_wage must fall between $25,000 and $250,000. Lower bound set below observed min for robustness; upper bound above BLS cap threshold.
rules.rationale: EDA: Actual range $30,160-$238,380. BLS cap is $239,200 (no capped wages in current data). Lower bound set to $25,000 (~$5K below observed min) and upper bound to $250,000 (~$12K above cap) for robustness against future data changes.

rules.name: median_annual_wage null count exactly 23
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN median_annual_wage IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 23 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.bls_ooh
rules.description: Exactly 23 occupations should have null median_annual_wage. These are physicians/surgeons, performers, dental specialists, and 1 fishing/hunting worker where BLS does not report wage data.
rules.rationale: EDA: 23 null-wage occupations confirmed. Breakdown: 14 physicians/surgeons, 5 performers, 3 dental specialists, 1 fishing/hunting. This is a known, stable set tied to BLS methodology.

rules.name: wage_available matches median_annual_wage nullity
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE wage_available != (median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL)
rules.description: wage_available must exactly equal (median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL). True for 809 rows, False for 23 rows.
rules.rationale: EDA: 809 non-null wages, 23 null wages. Logical model derivation: wage_available = (median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL). Must hold for all 832 rows.

rules.name: median_wage_capped: 0 True values
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE median_wage_capped = true
rules.description: No wages should be capped in the interactive export. The median_wage_capped flag exists for future-proofing but should be all False in current data.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'All False (832 of 832). Interactive export uses actual numeric wages, no top-coding.' Max observed wage $238,380 < BLS cap $239,200.

rules.name: wage_available not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE wage_available IS NULL
rules.description: wage_available is a derived NOT NULL boolean convenience flag.
rules.rationale: Logical model: wage_available is NOT NULL boolean. Derived from median_annual_wage nullity, always deterministic.

rules.name: median_wage_capped not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE median_wage_capped IS NULL
rules.description: median_wage_capped is a NOT NULL boolean preserved from Bronze.
rules.rationale: EDA: 0 nulls for median_wage_capped across 832 rows. Logical model marks as NOT NULL.

rules.name: education_code range 1-8
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE education_code IS NOT NULL AND (education_code < 1 OR education_code > 8)
rules.description: BLS education level codes must be in the range 1-8 when present (1=Doctoral through 8=No formal credential).
rules.rationale: EDA: Range 1-8, all 8 values present. 0 nulls across 832 rows. Domain context confirms BLS integer classification 1-8.

rules.name: work_experience_code range 1-3
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE work_experience_code IS NOT NULL AND (work_experience_code < 1 OR work_experience_code > 3)
rules.description: BLS work experience codes must be in the range 1-3 when present (1=5+ years, 2=Less than 5 years, 3=None).
rules.rationale: EDA: Range 1-3, all 3 values present. 0 nulls across 832 rows. Domain context confirms BLS integer classification 1-3.

rules.name: training_code range 1-6
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE training_code IS NOT NULL AND (training_code < 1 OR training_code > 6)
rules.description: BLS training codes must be in the range 1-6 when present (1=Internship/residency through 6=None).
rules.rationale: EDA: Range 1-6, all 6 values present. 0 nulls across 832 rows. Domain context confirms BLS integer classification 1-6.

rules.name: education_level_name referential integrity with education_code
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE education_code IS NOT NULL AND (education_level_name IS NULL OR education_level_name NOT IN ('Doctoral or professional degree', 'Master''s degree', 'Bachelor''s degree', 'Associate''s degree', 'Postsecondary nondegree award', 'Some college, no degree', 'High school diploma or equivalent', 'No formal educational credential'))
rules.description: When education_code is not null, education_level_name must be one of the 8 valid labels from the spec lookup table. Validates the derivation lookup.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'education_code maps deterministically 1:1 with education_typical across all 832 rows.' All 8 codes present. Spec defines the 8-row lookup table.

rules.name: employment_change consistency: projected - current = change
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE employment_current IS NOT NULL AND employment_projected IS NOT NULL AND employment_change IS NOT NULL AND ABS((employment_projected - employment_current) - employment_change) > 1000
rules.description: employment_projected minus employment_current should equal employment_change within a tolerance of +/- 1,000 (BLS rounding from thousands).
rules.rationale: EDA: 'employment_projected - employment_current = employment_change holds within +/- 1,000 rounding tolerance for all 832 rows. Zero violations.'

rules.name: source_load_date not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE source_load_date IS NULL
rules.description: source_load_date is a NOT NULL pipeline metadata field renamed from Bronze load_date.
rules.rationale: Logical model: source_load_date is NOT NULL. Renamed from Bronze load_date which has 0% null rate per EDA.

rules.name: ingested_at not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: ingested_at is a NOT NULL pipeline metadata timestamp generated at Silver transformation time.
rules.rationale: Logical model: ingested_at is NOT NULL. Generated at transformation time, must always be populated.

rules.name: growth_category not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_ooh WHERE growth_category IS NULL
rules.description: In current data, all 832 rows have non-null employment_change_pct, so growth_category should never be null. If future data has null pct values, this rule will need adjustment.
rules.rationale: EDA: 0 nulls for employment_change_pct across 832 rows, therefore 0 null growth_category values. All 832 rows assigned to one of 6 categories.

rules.name: All 22 SOC major groups represented
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT soc_major_group) = 22 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.bls_ooh
rules.description: All 22 SOC major groups must be present in the data. Missing groups indicate data loss during transformation.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'All 22 SOC major groups are populated. Group 51 (Production) is largest with 105 occupations. Group 23 (Legal) is smallest with 8.'
